The disease’s pathophysiology accounts for each organ system involved: necrotizing glomerulonephritis explains the renal findings; pulmonary capillaritis accounts for the respiratory distress and radiographic infiltrates; small‑vessel cerebral vasculitis produces the occipital infarcts; and leukocytoclastic vasculitis of the skin generates the necrotic papules.
